message: I am 54 and I was just diagnosed with breast cancer. It was caught early, which everyone keeps telling me is the good news. I had a lumpectomy a few weeks ago and they said the margins were clean and the lymph nodes they checked came back clear. They told me it is the ductal kind. Now my doctor is talking about the radiation step. I would like to know if there are any studies I could be part of that involve that radiation, somewhere around Phoenix or Scottsdale. I want to do everything right.

I have lung cancer that has spread to my bones. When I was diagnosed they told me it was the type with the EGFR mutation, and I have been taking a pill called Tagrisso, the osimertinib one, for almost two years. It was working and I finally felt like myself again. My last scan showed it is growing in a couple of new spots and my oncologist says the pill is starting to fail. I live near Phoenix and I am trying to figure out what trials might take me. I am honestly scared of running out of options.

My breast cancer is back. I beat it five years ago and I thought I was done. Now they tell me it came back and it is in my bones, and there are a couple of spots on my liver. For about a year and a half I was on hormone pills, letrozole, plus another one called palbociclib, and it was holding things steady, but the last scan showed it is on the move again. My doctor was clear that I have not had chemotherapy yet, just those pills. I do not really understand all of it. I just want to know if there is a study near Mesa that could help me, and what it would actually mean for me day to day.

profile.stage: Stage IV (metastatic, bone and liver metastases)
prior_treatments: Letrozole (aromatase inhibitor) plus palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor), approximately 18 months, now progressing

message: They found my lung cancer early, stage 1. I had surgery a couple of months ago to take out the lobe, and the surgeon said they got all of it. My scans since then have been clear, no sign of it anywhere else. My nephew keeps telling me I should get into a research study while I can. I am in Tucson and willing to drive up to Phoenix or Scottsdale. Is there a trial that would be right for someone in my situation.

profile.stage: Stage IA (pT1b N0 M0), fully resected, no evidence of disease

message: I was diagnosed with an aggressive prostate cancer about four months ago. It had not spread outside the pelvis, which my doctor said was good. I finished radiation about six weeks ago and I am on hormone shots, leuprolide. I am looking for any trial that might add to my treatment and lower the odds it comes back. One more thing, my sister had breast cancer when she was young, and my doctor mentioned I should think about genetic testing, but I have not done that yet. I am in Chandler. What is out there for me.

prior_treatments: Radiation therapy to the prostate, completed approximately 6 weeks ago
other_conditions: Family history: sister with breast cancer before age 50
